description: Machine-readable forensic verification ruleset for peptide Certificates of Analysis (COAs). Extracted from 9 reference articles covering COA authentication, HPLC/MS interpretation, document forensics, and vendor evaluation. 81 rules across 8 categories.

structure.name: Product Name Present
structure.description: COA must include the complete, unambiguous name of the peptide compound being analyzed.
structure.expected_value: Full compound name matching vendor label (e.g., 'BPC-157', 'Thymosin Beta-4', 'Semaglutide')
red_flags: Name absent
Vague descriptor like 'Peptide A' or 'Research Compound'
Name does not match product label
structure.evaluation_method: Check for clearly labeled compound name field; cross-reference against vendor product listing
structure.false_positive_notes: Custom or novel sequences may use internal code names — rule applies specifically to catalog peptides

structure.name: Batch or Lot Number Present
structure.description: Every COA must reference a specific, unique batch or lot number tying test results to a specific production run.
red_flags: No batch number
Generic placeholder like 'BATCH-001' or '123'
Batch number field exists but is blank
Single-digit or obviously sequential lot numbers across different peptide products
structure.evaluation_method: Verify batch number is present, non-generic, and matches the batch number on the product vial or packing slip
structure.false_positive_notes: Small vendors may use simple numbering schemes that are still legitimate; context and other indicators matter

structure.name: Analysis Date Present
structure.description: COA must include the date on which testing was performed.
structure.expected_value: Specific date in recognizable format (DD/MM/YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, or YYYY-MM-DD)
red_flags: No date listed
Date field present but empty
Vague timeframe like 'Q3 2024' with no specific date
structure.evaluation_method: Confirm a specific, parseable date is present
structure.false_positive_notes: None — a COA without a specific date cannot be verified regardless of other factors

structure.name: Testing Laboratory Name Present
structure.description: The COA must identify the specific laboratory that performed the testing.
red_flags: No lab name anywhere on document
'Internal Lab' or 'In-House QC'
'XYZ Research Internal Lab' with no independent identity
Logo without named legal entity
'Third-party tested' with no lab named
structure.evaluation_method: Check for a specific, named laboratory; attempt independent web search to verify lab exists
structure.false_positive_notes: None — lab name must be explicitly present

structure.name: Laboratory Address Present
structure.description: The COA should include a physical address for the testing laboratory.
structure.expected_value: Full or partial physical address including at minimum city and country
red_flags: No address
P.O. Box only with no street address
Address that cannot be verified via mapping services
structure.evaluation_method: Verify address presence; optionally cross-reference with lab's own website
structure.false_positive_notes: Some labs list only city and country in header — acceptable if lab is otherwise independently verifiable

structure.name: Laboratory Contact Information Present
structure.description: The COA should include contact details for the testing laboratory (phone, email, or website URL).
structure.expected_value: At least one contact method: phone number, email address, or website URL
red_flags: No contact information whatsoever
Generic email (gmail.com, yahoo.com) rather than lab domain email
structure.evaluation_method: Check for at least one legitimate contact method; attempt contact to verify lab identity
structure.false_positive_notes: Some accredited labs list only their website URL — acceptable if website independently verifiable

structure.name: Analyst Signature or Certification Present
structure.description: The COA should include an analyst signature, printed name, or laboratory director certification.
structure.expected_value: Handwritten signature, digital signature, printed analyst name, or authorized lab representative certification
red_flags: No signature or certification of any kind
Generic 'Approved By' field with no name
Signature appears identical across multiple COAs from different dates (copy-paste)
structure.evaluation_method: Check for a human signature or named certification; assess whether signature appears genuine vs. copy-pasted
structure.false_positive_notes: Electronic signature systems produce consistent signatures by design — this rule applies primarily to supposedly handwritten signatures

structure.name: Purity Result Present as Numerical Value
structure.description: The COA must include an HPLC purity percentage as a specific numerical value.
red_flags: No purity listed at all
'Purity: Pass' without a numerical percentage
'Purity: High' or similar qualitative descriptor
Purity field blank
structure.evaluation_method: Verify purity is reported as a specific numerical percentage, not a qualitative assessment
structure.false_positive_notes: None — numerical purity is mandatory for any credible analytical COA

structure.name: Mass Spectrometry Identity Confirmation Present
structure.description: The COA must include mass spectrometry results confirming compound identity via observed molecular weight.
structure.expected_value: Observed molecular weight value compared to theoretical MW for the stated peptide
red_flags: No MS data anywhere on document
Identity 'confirmed' only by compound name without mass verification
MS column present but blank or showing only 'Conforms'
structure.evaluation_method: Confirm MS-derived observed mass is present alongside theoretical mass for comparison
structure.false_positive_notes: Custom novel sequences may not have pre-calculated theoretical MW in literature — primarily applies to standard catalog peptides

structure.name: Pass or Fail Determination Present
structure.description: The COA should include an explicit Pass or Fail determination relative to a stated specification.
structure.expected_value: Explicit 'PASS' or 'FAIL' alongside specification (e.g., 'Purity: 98.3% — Spec: ≥98% — PASS')
red_flags: Results listed with no pass/fail judgment
Pass/Fail field present but empty
structure.evaluation_method: Check for explicit pass/fail language relative to stated specification
structure.false_positive_notes: Some legitimate lab formats list only results without explicit pass/fail language — absence alone is not disqualifying

structure.name: Sample Receipt and Test Date Present
structure.description: The COA should document when the sample was received by the lab and when testing was performed.
structure.expected_value: Sample receipt date and/or test date clearly stated as distinct from report issuance date
red_flags: Only 'report date' without analysis date
Single date with no clarification of what it represents
structure.evaluation_method: Verify chronological plausibility: receipt date ≤ test date ≤ report date
structure.false_positive_notes: Some labs combine receipt and test date into a single 'analysis date' field — acceptable

structure.name: Client or Supplier Name on COA
structure.description: The COA should identify the client or supplier who submitted the sample for testing.
structure.expected_value: Vendor or client name listed as sample submitter (e.g., 'Client: Honest Peptide')
red_flags: No client name anywhere on document
Client name does not match vendor from whom product was purchased
structure.evaluation_method: Verify client name matches vendor identity; flag mismatch as cross-reference failure
structure.false_positive_notes: Some labs omit client names under confidentiality agreements — absence alone is not disqualifying

numerical.name: HPLC Purity Meets Minimum Threshold
numerical.description: HPLC purity must meet minimum thresholds for the intended research application.
numerical.expected_value: ≥95% for standard RUO research; ≥98% for advanced quantitative research; ≥99% for pharmaceutical-aspirant grade
red_flags: Purity < 95% for any research-grade claim
Purity < 98% when vendor claims 'pharmaceutical grade' or 'premium'
Purity < 90% in any context
numerical.evaluation_method: Parse purity percentage; flag below 95% as major concern; flag below 90% as critical rejection
numerical.false_positive_notes: Early-stage exploratory research may accept 85-90%; crude peptides exist for specific assay types — context-dependent

numerical.name: Purity Value Has Specific Non-Round Decimal
numerical.description: Genuine HPLC purity results are reported to one or two decimal places and show natural instrument variation — not suspiciously round numbers.
numerical.expected_value: Values like 98.3%, 99.1%, 97.8% — specific, non-round decimal values
red_flags: Purity reported as exactly 99.0% or 100.0%
All products from vendor showing identical purity to the decimal
Purity reported as integer with no decimal (e.g., '99%')
numerical.evaluation_method: Check decimal precision; evaluate pattern across multiple COAs from same vendor for suspicious uniformity
numerical.false_positive_notes: A single round number on a single COA is inconclusive; the pattern across a vendor's catalog is the key indicator

numerical.name: Purity Does Not Exceed 100%
numerical.description: HPLC purity cannot physically exceed 100%. Any reported value above 100% indicates data fabrication.
red_flags: Any purity value > 100%
numerical.evaluation_method: Simple numerical check; any value exceeding 100.0% is an automatic forgery indicator

numerical.name: Mass Spectrometry Error Within Instrument Tolerance
numerical.description: The difference between observed and theoretical molecular weight must fall within instrument-appropriate tolerances.
numerical.expected_value: MALDI-TOF: ±0.1–0.3 Da for peptides <2000 Da; ESI-MS: ±0.05 Da; general tolerance ±1 Da
red_flags: Mass error > 1 Da (general threshold)
Mass error > 0.3 Da for MALDI-TOF on small peptides
Observed mass exactly equals theoretical mass with zero error (see FORG-003)
numerical.evaluation_method: Calculate |observed_mass - theoretical_mass|; compare to instrument-appropriate tolerance; note method used
numerical.false_positive_notes: ESI-MS calculates mass from charge states — averaging can shift apparent error; method must be known to determine appropriate tolerance

numerical.name: Net Peptide Content Within Expected Range
numerical.description: Net Peptide Content (NPC) measures actual peptide percentage in total powder, accounting for counter-ions (TFA, acetate, HCl) and residual moisture. Should fall within 60–90%.
red_flags: NPC > 95% (chemically implausible for lyophilized peptides with counter-ions and moisture)
NPC < 50% (suggests excessive counter-ion or moisture content)
NPC = 100% (physically impossible)
NPC value identical to HPLC purity (suggests the two metrics are being confused)
numerical.evaluation_method: Parse NPC value; flag values outside 50–95% range as suspicious; values >95% should be treated as critical forgery flag
numerical.false_positive_notes: Short di/tripeptides may have NPC outside typical range; the NPC determination method affects results

numerical.name: COA Age Within Acceptable Window
numerical.description: The analysis date should be recent enough to be relevant to current stock.
numerical.expected_value: Analysis date within 18 months of purchase or evaluation date; ideally within 90 days
red_flags: Analysis date > 24 months before current date
Analysis date > 18 months for actively marketed product
Single old COA provided for new orders as if it applies to all inventory
numerical.evaluation_method: Calculate days between analysis date and current date; flag if >548 days (18 months); critical concern if >730 days (24 months)
numerical.false_positive_notes: Long-shelf-life compounds tested at synthesis may have older COAs for legitimate old stock batches

numerical.name: HPLC Detection Wavelength is Standard for Peptides
numerical.description: Peptide purity by HPLC should be measured at standard UV detection wavelengths for amide bond absorption.
numerical.expected_value: Detection wavelength at 214 nm or 220 nm (primary peptide bond UV absorption)
red_flags: Detection at 254 nm without justification (typical for aromatic chromophores, not general peptides)
No wavelength specified in HPLC method
numerical.evaluation_method: Check stated detection wavelength; flag use of non-standard wavelengths without explanation
numerical.false_positive_notes: Peptides containing aromatic residues (Trp, Tyr, Phe) may use 280 nm — acceptable with justification; 210 nm also acceptable

numerical.name: Trace Impurity Peaks Present in HPLC Chromatogram
numerical.description: Real HPLC chromatograms show small impurity peaks even in high-purity samples. A completely clean chromatogram with zero secondary peaks is suspicious.
numerical.expected_value: Minor secondary peaks visible, totaling 0.1–5% area for samples claiming 95–99% purity
red_flags: Zero impurity peaks visible in chromatogram while purity claimed at <100%
Chromatogram shows only a single perfect Gaussian peak
Integration table showing 100% area for main peak when purity listed as 98% or 99%
numerical.evaluation_method: Examine chromatogram for presence of impurity peaks; flag complete absence when purity claimed below 99.9%
numerical.false_positive_notes: Very high purity (>99.8%) may show negligible impurity peaks; low-resolution chromatogram images may hide minor peaks

numerical.name: Endotoxin Level Meets Standard Threshold
numerical.description: For injectable-grade peptides, bacterial endotoxin testing should meet standard thresholds.
numerical.expected_value: Endotoxin < 0.5 EU/mg (standard injectable threshold); result in EU/ml or EU/vial with volume stated
red_flags: Endotoxin level > 0.5 EU/mg
Endotoxin listed as 'Not Tested' for products marketed with injection-adjacent language
Units not specified
numerical.evaluation_method: Parse endotoxin value and units; convert to EU/mg if possible; flag values exceeding threshold
numerical.false_positive_notes: RUO peptides not intended for injection do not require endotoxin testing — absence is expected in basic RUO COAs

numerical.name: Known Catalog Peptide Molecular Weight in Reference Range
numerical.description: Commonly tested catalog peptides have well-established theoretical molecular weights that the COA observed mass should closely match.
numerical.expected_value: BPC-157: ~1419.5 Da; TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4): ~4963 Da; GHK-Cu peptide component: ~340 Da
red_flags: BPC-157 observed mass significantly different from ~1419 Da
TB-500 observed mass significantly different from ~4963 Da
Any standard catalog peptide with mass discrepancy > 2 Da from known theoretical value
numerical.evaluation_method: Look up theoretical MW for the specific peptide; compare to observed MS value; flag discrepancies beyond instrument tolerance
numerical.false_positive_notes: Salt adducts (Na+22, K+38) can shift apparent mass in certain MS modes; ESI shows charge-state-divided m/z values — calculate correctly

numerical.name: HPLC Flow Rate Within Analytical Range
numerical.description: HPLC method parameters should include a plausible flow rate for analytical-scale peptide separation.
numerical.expected_value: Flow rate between 0.5 and 1.5 mL/min for standard analytical-scale HPLC
red_flags: Flow rate > 5 mL/min (preparative scale, not analytical)
Flow rate < 0.1 mL/min (unusually slow for standard HPLC)
numerical.evaluation_method: Parse flow rate from method parameters; flag values outside typical analytical range
numerical.false_positive_notes: UHPLC systems operate at 0.2–0.5 mL/min legitimately; minor deviation acceptable — this is a plausibility check, not a hard cutoff

analytical_methods.name: HPLC Method Explicitly Named
analytical_methods.description: HPLC is the industry standard for peptide purity analysis. COA must explicitly reference HPLC as the purity testing method.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Explicit reference to HPLC (High-Performance Liquid Chromatography) as purity analysis method
red_flags: No method specified for purity result
Purity stated without any analytical method named
Purity claimed via spectrophotometry alone without HPLC
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Confirm 'HPLC' is explicitly named as purity analysis method in method section or results table
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: None — HPLC is mandatory for credible purity claims on research peptides

analytical_methods.name: Mass Spectrometry Method Explicitly Named
analytical_methods.description: MS testing is required to confirm molecular identity. HPLC alone cannot confirm the compound is correct.
analytical_methods.expected_value: MS, ESI-MS, MALDI-TOF, or equivalent method explicitly named for identity confirmation
red_flags: Identity 'confirmed' only by HPLC retention time with no MS
No MS data present for catalog peptides
'Identity: Conforms' without any molecular weight data
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Verify MS-derived molecular weight data is present; distinguish MS identity data from HPLC purity data
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: NMR is an accepted supplementary identity method; MS remains the primary standard for catalog peptides

analytical_methods.name: Reversed-Phase HPLC with C18 Column
analytical_methods.description: Standard peptide purity analysis uses RP-HPLC with a C18 stationary phase column.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Column type specified as C18 or octadecylsilica (ODS); RP-HPLC method stated
red_flags: Column type not specified anywhere in method
Ion-exchange or non-reversed-phase column without justification
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check method section for column type specification; note if non-C18 without justification
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: C4 or C8 columns are legitimately used for large hydrophobic peptides — acceptable deviation with justification

analytical_methods.name: Mobile Phase Specification Present
analytical_methods.description: HPLC mobile phase should be specified. Standard for peptides is a water/acetonitrile gradient with TFA or formate modifier.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Mobile phase components listed (e.g., 'A: 0.1% TFA in water; B: 0.1% TFA in acetonitrile')
red_flags: No mobile phase specified at all
Mobile phase incompatible with UV detection at 214/220 nm
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check for mobile phase specification in method parameters section
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Formate, phosphate, or ammonium acetate buffers are acceptable alternatives to TFA — exact composition is less critical than presence

analytical_methods.name: MS Technique Type Specified
analytical_methods.description: The type of mass spectrometry used should be specified to allow appropriate mass error tolerance assessment.
analytical_methods.expected_value: MS technique explicitly named: MALDI-TOF, ESI-MS, ESI-QTOF, Q-TOF, or similar
red_flags: MS data present but technique type not named anywhere
'Mass confirmed' without naming any technique
Only 'MS' listed with no elaboration
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check for explicit MS technique name in method section or results table
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Some COA formats list MS results without naming technique — request clarification from vendor if evaluating for high-stakes use

analytical_methods.name: HPLC Chromatogram Data Attached
analytical_methods.description: A credible HPLC COA should include the actual chromatogram or peak table data, not just a purity number.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Visual chromatogram image or peak table data with retention times and area percentages
red_flags: Purity percentage stated without any supporting chromatogram
Chromatogram field labeled but blank
No peak integration table provided
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check for presence of chromatogram image or peak table; a purity number alone without supporting data is insufficient
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Some established labs (notably Janoshik Analytical) provide numerical results without embedded chromatogram images — absent chromatogram is not disqualifying if lab is independently verifiable and digital verification key is provided

analytical_methods.name: HPLC Chromatogram Baseline Stability
analytical_methods.description: HPLC chromatogram baseline should be flat and stable, indicating proper equipment calibration and sample preparation.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Flat, stable baseline before and after main peptide peak
red_flags: Significant monotonic baseline drift throughout entire run
Baseline rises sharply at gradient end (may indicate column bleed or contamination)
Periodic noise pattern throughout baseline (instrument malfunction)
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Visual inspection of chromatogram baseline for stability across the run
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Minor drift in gradient elution systems is normal; concern is pronounced instability or non-monotonic drift

analytical_methods.name: Integration Lines Visible on Chromatogram
analytical_methods.description: Properly analyzed HPLC chromatograms show integration markers at peak boundaries, indicating software-processed rather than manually drawn data.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Integration start and end markers visible on peaks in chromatogram
red_flags: No integration lines visible on any peaks while area percentages are claimed
Peak areas claimed without any visible integration boundary markers
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Visual inspection of chromatogram image for integration markers on peaks
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Some software does not display integration marks visually in exported chromatograms — absence alone is weak evidence

analytical_methods.name: ESI-MS Charge States Present for Large Peptides
analytical_methods.description: For peptides >1000 Da analyzed by ESI-MS, multiple charge states should be present in the spectrum. Each state should yield the same calculated mass.
analytical_methods.expected_value: Multiple charge state peaks ([M+H]+, [M+2H]2+, [M+3H]3+, etc.) for ESI-MS of peptides >1000 Da
red_flags: Single charge state peak only for large peptide analyzed by ESI-MS
Multiple charge states present but yielding inconsistent calculated masses
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check for multiple charge state peaks in ESI spectrum for large peptides; verify mass calculations from each state are internally consistent
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: MALDI-TOF typically shows predominantly singly charged ions — charge state multiplicity requirement applies specifically to ESI-MS, not MALDI

analytical_methods.name: Net Peptide Content Method Specified
analytical_methods.description: If NPC is reported, the method of determination should be specified.
analytical_methods.expected_value: NPC determination method specified (e.g., amino acid analysis, nitrogen determination, UV at 205 nm)
red_flags: NPC stated without any method specified
NPC value identical to HPLC purity percentage (indicates confusion of two distinct metrics)
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check if NPC is reported; if so, verify method is specified and value differs from HPLC purity
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: NPC is optional for basic RUO COAs; absence of NPC is not a red flag — it is reporting NPC without a method that raises concern

analytical_methods.name: Heavy Metals Testing by ICP-MS
analytical_methods.description: Comprehensive COAs should include heavy metals screening using ICP-MS for Pb, Cd, As, Hg.
analytical_methods.expected_value: ICP-MS heavy metals results for at minimum Pb, Cd, As, Hg at ppb-level detection
red_flags: Heavy metals listed as 'Not Detected' without specifying the method or detection limit
Heavy metals panel absent for products with pharmaceutical-grade claims
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Check for ICP-MS heavy metals data; note absence as incomplete documentation for premium claims
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: RUO peptides are not required to test heavy metals; absence in basic COAs is expected and not a red flag by itself

analytical_methods.name: Endotoxin Testing by LAL Assay
analytical_methods.description: If endotoxin testing is performed, it should use the Limulus Amebocyte Lysate (LAL) assay or accepted equivalent.
analytical_methods.expected_value: LAL assay method cited for endotoxin results; results expressed in EU/ml, EU/vial, or EU/mg
red_flags: Endotoxin result claimed without specifying any test method
Method described that is not LAL or rFC (Recombinant Factor C)
analytical_methods.evaluation_method: Verify LAL or rFC is cited as endotoxin method if endotoxin testing is included
analytical_methods.false_positive_notes: Recombinant Factor C (rFC) assay is a fully accepted alternative to LAL — not a red flag

lab_credentials.name: Third-Party Independent Laboratory Used
lab_credentials.description: Testing must be performed by an independent laboratory with no financial or operational relationship with the vendor.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Testing performed by named independent third-party laboratory
red_flags: In-house testing by vendor
'Internal QC lab' designation
Lab owned or operated by vendor
Vendor explicitly states they test their own products
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Verify independence of testing lab from vendor; check for disclosed financial relationship
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: In-house COAs are better than none but should receive substantially lower scores; independence is the gold standard and cannot be substituted

lab_credentials.name: ISO/IEC 17025 Accreditation Stated
lab_credentials.description: The testing laboratory should hold ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation, the international standard for testing laboratory competence.
lab_credentials.expected_value: ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation explicitly stated; accreditation body named
red_flags: No accreditation mentioned
'ISO certified laboratory' used (incorrect terminology — labs are accredited not certified)
Accreditation body named but non-existent or unverifiable
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Check for ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation language; verify accreditation body is legitimate; search accreditation body database
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Some legitimate specialized peptide testing labs (e.g., Janoshik Analytical) are widely trusted in the community despite not always prominently displaying accreditation — combine with LAB-006 and LAB-007

lab_credentials.name: Correct Accreditation Terminology Used
lab_credentials.description: Testing laboratories are 'accredited' under ISO/IEC 17025, not 'certified'. Consistent terminology misuse is a credibility signal.
lab_credentials.expected_value: 'Accredited' used in context of laboratory quality standard compliance
red_flags: 'ISO 17025 certified laboratory'
'Certified analytical lab' when referring to laboratory quality system status
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Check accreditation language; note consistent misuse of 'certified' vs 'accredited' for quality systems
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Lay terminology misuse is common and not necessarily indicative of fraud; this is a low-weight credibility indicator only

lab_credentials.name: Accreditation Scope Covers Peptide Analysis
lab_credentials.description: The laboratory's accreditation scope must cover the analytical methods used (HPLC, MS) for peptide testing.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Lab accreditation scope includes HPLC purity analysis and/or mass spectrometry for pharmaceutical or peptide compounds
red_flags: Lab accredited only for environmental testing or food safety
Accreditation scope does not include pharmaceutical or small molecule analysis
Scope of accreditation not disclosed or unverifiable
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Search accrediting body online database for lab's scope; verify it covers peptide analytical methods
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Scope databases may not be easily searchable by public; inability to verify scope online is not automatic disqualification

lab_credentials.name: Accreditation Body is Recognized and Verifiable
lab_credentials.description: The stated accreditation body must be a real, recognized national or international accreditation authority.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Recognized accreditation body: ANAB (US), A2LA (US), UKAS (UK), CNAS (China), DAkkS (Germany), COFRAC (France), NATA (Australia), or ILAC member body
red_flags: Unknown accreditation body not appearing in ILAC membership directory
Body name that resembles but is not an official entity (e.g., 'World Accreditation Bureau')
Fabricated-sounding accreditation body
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Search for accreditation body name; verify it is a member of ILAC (International Laboratory Accreditation Cooperation)
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: None — a fabricated or unrecognized accreditation body is an unambiguous red flag

lab_credentials.name: Lab Independently Discoverable via Web Search
lab_credentials.description: A legitimate analytical laboratory should be independently findable via web search with consistent identity information.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Lab has established web presence; contact information consistent between COA and web presence
red_flags: Lab name returns zero independent search results
Only results linking back to the vendor's own website
Lab website is newly created (no historical footprint)
Lab information inconsistent between COA and online presence
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Web search for lab name; verify independent existence; check Wayback Machine for historical presence if needed
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Small or specialized regional labs may have minimal web presence; absence alone is inconclusive — combine with LAB-007 direct contact verification

lab_credentials.name: Lab Verifies COA When Contacted Directly
lab_credentials.description: When contacted, the testing laboratory should be able to confirm the COA is genuine and that the report number exists in their system.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Lab confirms COA authenticity when contacted with report or task number
red_flags: Lab states they have never worked with the named vendor
Lab has no record of the specified batch or task number
Lab denies ever issuing the document
Lab contact information is unreachable by any method
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Contact lab via phone or email with COA report/task number; ask lab to confirm report existence (not necessarily vendor identity)
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Labs may not disclose client names under confidentiality agreements — they can confirm report number existence without naming the client

lab_credentials.name: Digital Verification Key or QR Code is Valid
lab_credentials.description: Some labs (notably Janoshik Analytical) provide digital verification keys or QR codes linking to an online database confirming COA authenticity.
lab_credentials.expected_value: QR code or verification key links to lab verification system and displays test data matching the paper COA
red_flags: QR code leads to vendor website rather than lab verification system
Verification key returns no result or server error
QR code links to a domain unrelated to the stated lab
Verification key format does not match lab's documented format
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Scan QR code or enter verification key at lab verification URL (e.g., janoshik.com/verify); confirm all data matches paper COA
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Not all legitimate labs offer digital verification; absence of QR code or verification key is not a red flag unless the lab specifically claims to provide it

lab_credentials.name: Lab Has Established Track Record in Peptide Testing
lab_credentials.description: Testing by a laboratory with known history in peptide analytical testing provides higher confidence than an unknown lab.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Testing performed by a recognized peptide testing laboratory: Janoshik Analytical (Slovakia; verify at janoshik.com/verify), Colmaric Analyticals (USA), Vanguard Laboratory (A2LA #6377.01.01), MZ Biolabs, Freedom Analytics Labs (USA), Novascreen Biosciences
red_flags: Completely unknown lab with no verifiable history in peptide testing community
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Cross-reference lab name against known legitimate peptide testing laboratories; unknown labs require additional verification steps
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: New or regional labs may be entirely legitimate; this is a trust-building indicator, not a disqualifying check in isolation

lab_credentials.name: Accreditation is Current (Not Expired)
lab_credentials.description: ISO 17025 accreditation must be current as of the analysis date. Expired accreditation provides no quality assurance.
lab_credentials.expected_value: Accreditation is current as of analysis date; any accreditation expiry shown on COA is after the analysis date
red_flags: Accreditation expiry date shown is before COA analysis date
Accreditation certificate displayed on vendor site with past expiry date
Accreditation body database shows accreditation as lapsed
lab_credentials.evaluation_method: Check accreditation expiry date on COA or via accreditation body's online directory; compare to analysis date
lab_credentials.false_positive_notes: Most COAs do not list accreditation expiry; online accreditation body databases are the authoritative verification source

formatting.name: Document Clarity and Professional Appearance
formatting.description: Legitimate COAs are generated by professional laboratory information management systems and should be clear, readable, and professional in appearance.
formatting.expected_value: High-resolution, clearly legible text and graphics throughout the document
red_flags: Blurry or pixelated overall document at normal reading zoom
Text that becomes unclear when zoomed to 150%
Obvious scan artifacts on a document that should be digitally native
formatting.evaluation_method: Zoom to 200% in PDF viewer; check for overall clarity and professional appearance across all sections
formatting.false_positive_notes: Older labs may provide scanned paper copies; low resolution alone is not disqualifying if other elements are consistent and lab is verifiable

formatting.name: Uniform Resolution Across All Document Sections
formatting.description: All sections of a genuine digital COA should have consistent resolution. Mixed resolution between sections indicates image editing and content replacement.
formatting.expected_value: Consistent resolution and sharpness across all text, numbers, and graphics throughout the document
red_flags: Some sections crisp while specific data fields appear pixelated
Numbers in result fields at visibly different resolution than surrounding text
Lab logo noticeably sharper or blurrier than document body text
Signature area at different resolution than surrounding content
formatting.evaluation_method: Compare resolution of key sections at same zoom level: logo area, result numbers, signatures, and body text
formatting.false_positive_notes: Embedded raster graphics (e.g., lab logo as PNG) may naturally differ slightly from surrounding vector text — minor variation in logo area is acceptable

formatting.name: Font Consistency Throughout Document
formatting.description: A genuine lab document uses consistent fonts throughout. Data fields in a noticeably different font suggest content was inserted from another source.
formatting.expected_value: Consistent font family, size, and style used for similar content types throughout the document
red_flags: Purity or mass result fields in noticeably different font than column labels
Numbers in data fields at different typeface than surrounding text
Inconsistent character spacing or kerning specifically in numerical result fields
formatting.evaluation_method: Compare fonts used in result data fields against document template text and headers; flag obvious mismatches
formatting.false_positive_notes: Multi-column lab software may use slightly different formatting for data vs. label fields by design; minor stylistic difference is not equivalent to different font family

formatting.name: Text and Number Alignment Consistent with Template
formatting.description: In a genuine lab-generated document, all data values align consistently with the document's column headers and template grid.
formatting.expected_value: Numbers and text align properly with column headers and template structure
red_flags: Numerical values that appear slightly offset from their labeled fields
Text baseline inconsistency within the same table row
Values that do not align with corresponding column headers above them
formatting.evaluation_method: Check alignment of data values against template structure; zoom in to 200% to check baseline and column alignment
formatting.false_positive_notes: OCR-extracted or reformatted documents may have minor alignment issues that are not intentional; pattern of misalignment is more significant than isolated instance

formatting.name: No Clone-Stamp or Copy-Paste Artifacts Visible
formatting.description: Photo editing tools leave detectable artifacts when content is cloned or copy-pasted over original content to alter numerical values.
formatting.expected_value: No repeating texture patterns, no visible seam lines, no areas of unusual uniformity in document background
red_flags: Repeating texture or noise patterns in background regions near data fields
Visible straight-edge seam lines in background image areas
Background texture discontinuity immediately surrounding specific text or numbers
Areas of suspiciously uniform background where document texture should show natural variation
formatting.evaluation_method: Inspect background regions near key data fields for repeating patterns; check areas around numerical values for unusual uniformity inconsistent with rest of document
formatting.false_positive_notes: Pure white or solid-color backgrounds will naturally appear uniform — look for texture inconsistencies specifically in scanned or printed documents with visible paper texture

formatting.name: Signature Appears Natural and Unique
formatting.description: If a handwritten or digital signature is present, it should appear natural and should vary across different documents if the analyst signs regularly.
formatting.expected_value: Natural-looking signature with organic line quality; some variation present across multiple COAs from same analyst
red_flags: Signature appears pixel-for-pixel identical across multiple COAs from different dates
Signature appears digitally drawn with perfectly uniform stroke weight
Signature shows jagged or pixelated edges inconsistent with surrounding document quality
formatting.evaluation_method: Compare signature across multiple COAs from same lab; check for organic variation; zoom in to examine stroke quality
formatting.false_positive_notes: Electronic signature systems produce consistent signatures by design — identical electronic signatures across COAs from same analyst are normal; rule applies primarily to supposedly handwritten signatures

formatting.name: Security Features Appear Integrated, Not Overlaid
formatting.description: Security features (watermarks, QR codes, seals, stamps) should appear as integral document elements rather than obvious post-creation overlays.
formatting.expected_value: Security features appear as embedded document elements with appropriate transparency and integration
red_flags: QR code appears as a floating rectangular image overlay with visible white background box
Watermark with sharp distinct edges inconsistent with typical transparency
Lab seal or stamp that appears pasted with obvious rectangular boundary
formatting.evaluation_method: Examine security features for signs of post-creation insertion; check layer properties in PDF viewer if accessible
formatting.false_positive_notes: Digital-native COAs may legitimately embed QR codes as image objects with white backgrounds — this is a weak indicator only

formatting.name: Document Fully Downloadable and Accessible
formatting.description: Legitimate COAs should be downloadable as full-resolution files. Non-downloadable or partial COA display prevents proper verification.
formatting.expected_value: COA available as downloadable PDF or high-resolution image with all content readable when zoomed
red_flags: COA only viewable as embedded low-resolution thumbnail with no download option
Download button absent or non-functional
Content becomes unreadable when zoomed to normal inspection magnification
COA exists only as a non-zoomable screenshot on vendor website
formatting.evaluation_method: Attempt to download COA; verify all content is readable at full resolution
formatting.false_positive_notes: Some vendors provide COA by email on request rather than public posting — acceptable if provided promptly and completely on request

metadata.name: PDF Creation Date Consistent with Analysis Date
metadata.description: PDF document metadata creation date should be on or after the analysis date stated in the document body.
metadata.expected_value: PDF creation date >= analysis date stated in document
red_flags: PDF creation date significantly predates the analysis date (document existed before testing occurred)
PDF creation date is years later than stated analysis date (old COA data repackaged in new document)
metadata.evaluation_method: Extract PDF metadata via File > Properties in Adobe Reader or exiftool; compare 'Creation Date' to analysis date in document
metadata.false_positive_notes: Documents scanned to PDF, template-based generation, or system clock errors can cause date discrepancies — not automatically disqualifying but warrants investigation

metadata.name: Authoring Software is Professional Lab or Document Tool
metadata.description: PDF metadata should indicate the document was created by professional laboratory or office software, not image editing applications.
metadata.expected_value: PDF Producer or Creator field shows professional software: Adobe Acrobat, LIMS software, Empower, Chromeleon, LibreOffice, Microsoft Word converted to PDF
red_flags: PDF created by Adobe Photoshop
PDF created by GIMP
PDF created by Microsoft Paint or any raster image editor
PDF created by any photo manipulation software
metadata.evaluation_method: Check PDF metadata 'Producer' and 'Creator' fields; flag any image editing software as a strong forgery indicator
metadata.false_positive_notes: Some labs scan paper COAs and convert to PDF using image-to-PDF tools — context matters; image-editor-originated PDFs are still a significant red flag requiring additional verification

metadata.name: No Future-Dated Analysis
metadata.description: Analysis date must not be in the future relative to the current date. Future-dated analysis is physically impossible.
metadata.expected_value: Analysis date <= current date (2026-05-17 at time of this ruleset)
red_flags: Analysis date set to any future date
Test date listed as upcoming
Any date field set to a future date
metadata.evaluation_method: Parse analysis date and compare to today's date; any future date is an automatic forgery indicator
metadata.false_positive_notes: None — a future analysis date is physically impossible; constitutes clear evidence of document fabrication

metadata.name: Document Modification History Clean
metadata.description: PDF metadata should not show unexpected edits made long after the document was generated.
metadata.expected_value: Modification date equals or is close to creation date; no extensive revision history
red_flags: Document modified significantly (weeks or months) after initial creation
Multiple revision versions in PDF metadata history
Modification date much later than creation date without legitimate explanation
metadata.evaluation_method: Check PDF metadata 'Modified Date' vs 'Creation Date'; note gaps longer than a few hours
metadata.false_positive_notes: PDF optimization, printing to new PDF, digital signature application, and accessibility tagging can all update modification date legitimately

metadata.name: COA is Batch-Specific, Not Generic Template
metadata.description: Each COA should contain unique batch-specific data indicating it was generated specifically for that production run, not reused from a template with only the product name changed.
metadata.expected_value: COA contains batch-specific values (unique lot number, specific purity percentage, test date for that batch) indicating it was generated fresh for this production run
red_flags: Structurally identical COA documents across different batches with only product name changed
Same PDF metadata creation timestamp across COAs for supposedly different test events
Same analyst signature date appearing across COAs from different stated test dates
Purity values identical to decimal point across multiple supposedly independent batches
metadata.evaluation_method: Compare COAs for different products from same vendor; flag structurally and numerically identical content across chemically distinct products
metadata.false_positive_notes: Template-generated COAs are legitimate when batch-specific data fields are properly populated with distinct values; flag only when identical content appears across supposedly different batches

cross_reference.name: Batch Number Matches Product Vial
cross_reference.description: The lot or batch number on the COA must exactly match the lot or batch number on the product vial label and packing slip.
cross_reference.expected_value: Exact character-for-character match between COA batch number and vial/order documentation
red_flags: Batch number on COA differs from batch number on vial
No batch number on vial to compare against
Vendor provides only a generic COA not specific to the received batch
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Compare COA lot number field directly to vial label and packing slip; flag any discrepancy
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: None — a batch number mismatch means the COA may not represent the product actually received

cross_reference.name: Observed MS Mass Matches Theoretical for Named Peptide
cross_reference.description: The observed molecular weight from MS must match the theoretical molecular weight calculated from the peptide's stated amino acid sequence within instrument tolerance.
cross_reference.expected_value: Observed mass within instrument-appropriate tolerance of independently calculated theoretical mass for the named peptide sequence
red_flags: Mass discrepancy > 1 Da between observed and theoretical
Theoretical mass not provided for comparison
Observed mass matches the theoretical mass of a different known peptide (wrong compound)
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Calculate theoretical MW from amino acid sequence independently; compare to observed MS value; flag discrepancies beyond instrument tolerance
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Counter-ion adducts (Na+22 Da, K+38 Da) can shift apparent mass in certain MS modes; ESI-MS shows charge-state-divided m/z values requiring deconvolution

cross_reference.name: Vendor Name on COA Matches Known Vendor
cross_reference.description: The client or submitter name on the COA should match the vendor from whom the product was purchased.
cross_reference.expected_value: Client name on COA matches vendor's registered name, trade name, or known DBA
red_flags: Client name on COA is a completely different unrelated company
Client listed as generic entity with no connection to vendor
No client name present preventing any vendor identity verification
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Cross-reference COA client name against vendor's registered business name, website name, and known trade names
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Vendors may test under parent company name, LLC name, or operating subsidiary — minor naming variants that are clearly related are acceptable

cross_reference.name: All Dates Chronologically Plausible
cross_reference.description: All dates on the COA must form a chronologically plausible sequence: sample receipt ≤ analysis date ≤ report date ≤ current date.
cross_reference.expected_value: Sample receipt date ≤ analysis date ≤ report date; all dates in the past
red_flags: Analysis date listed before sample receipt date
Report date listed before analysis date
Any date in the future (see also META-003)
Dates that are logically impossible as a sequence
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Extract all dates from COA; verify chronological sequence; flag any logical impossibility
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Time zone differences may cause minor apparent discrepancies of hours; report dates matching analysis dates exactly are common for same-day report generation

cross_reference.name: ESI-MS Charge States Internally Consistent
cross_reference.description: For ESI-MS data, multiple charge states should all independently calculate to the same monoisotopic or average mass.
cross_reference.expected_value: Mass calculated independently from each charge state peak agrees with all others to within ±0.1 Da
red_flags: Charge states calculating to significantly different masses (>0.5 Da difference)
One charge state consistent with expected peptide while others calculate to inconsistent values
cross_reference.evaluation_method: For each charge state peak m/z: calculate mass = (m/z × z) − (z × 1.00728); verify all results agree to within tolerance
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Adduct peaks (Na+, K+) will calculate to different masses — ensure adducts are identified and excluded from mass calculation before evaluating consistency

cross_reference.name: MS Adduct Peaks at Expected Mass Offsets
cross_reference.description: If adduct peaks are present in the MS spectrum, they should appear at chemically predictable offsets from the main molecular ion peak.
cross_reference.expected_value: Na+ adduct at M+22 Da; K+ adduct at M+38 Da; TFA adduct at M+114 Da from main ion peak
red_flags: Peaks at unexpected offsets not corresponding to any known adduct or isotope
Satellite peaks claimed as adducts that do not match expected adduct masses
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Calculate expected adduct positions from main peak; verify any satellite peaks match known adduct masses
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Not all spectra show adduct peaks; absence of adduct peaks is entirely normal and not a red flag

cross_reference.name: QR Code or Verification Key Data Matches Paper COA
cross_reference.description: If a digital verification system is present, all data returned by the verification system must exactly match the paper COA content.
cross_reference.expected_value: Online verification system returns identical compound name, batch number, purity, and dates as stated on paper COA
red_flags: Online verification shows different purity value than paper COA
Online system shows different batch number than paper COA
Online system has no record of the verification key at all
QR code leads to a different lab's website than stated on COA
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Scan QR code or enter verification key at lab's verification URL; compare every data field between online result and paper COA
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: None — any discrepancy between digital verification and paper COA data is unambiguous evidence of document tampering

cross_reference.name: HPLC and MS Data Reference Same Batch
cross_reference.description: HPLC purity data and MS identity data on the same COA should both reference the same batch number and analysis date.
cross_reference.expected_value: Same batch or lot number referenced in both HPLC purity section and MS identity section of the COA
red_flags: HPLC and MS sections reference different batch numbers
Significantly different analysis dates for HPLC and MS sections without documented explanation
cross_reference.evaluation_method: Compare batch numbers and dates in HPLC section vs MS section of the same COA document
cross_reference.false_positive_notes: Some labs run HPLC and MS analyses on different instruments with slightly staggered dates — minor date difference (same day or next day) is acceptable if batch numbers match

forgery_indicators.name: Suspiciously Round Purity Values
forgery_indicators.description: Fabricated COAs frequently show exactly round purity values. Real HPLC instrument output produces specific decimal values.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Purity values with specific non-round decimals (e.g., 98.3%, 99.1%, 97.8%)
red_flags: Purity exactly 99.00%
Purity exactly 100.00%
All products from vendor showing identical purity to the decimal point
Purity value landing exactly on a common threshold (exactly 98.00%, exactly 99.00%)
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Parse purity value; flag if exactly round (N.00%); evaluate cross-product pattern across vendor's catalog
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: A single round number on a single COA is plausible; the critical indicator is the pattern of uniformly round numbers across a vendor's entire catalog

forgery_indicators.name: Zero Impurity Peaks in Sub-100% Purity Claim
forgery_indicators.description: A chromatogram showing zero secondary peaks while claiming purity below 100% is a physical impossibility and indicates fabrication.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Non-zero impurity peaks present in chromatogram when purity claimed is below 99.9%
red_flags: Single perfect peak with zero secondary peaks while purity claimed at 98% or 99%
Integration table showing 100.00% area for main peak when purity is stated as 98% or 99%
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: If chromatogram is provided, check for at least minor impurity peaks; absence with <99.9% purity claim is a critical red flag
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Very high purity (>99.8%) may genuinely show visually negligible secondary peaks; image resolution limitations may make minor peaks invisible in small embedded images

forgery_indicators.name: Exact Theoretical MS Match With Zero Error
forgery_indicators.description: An observed MS mass exactly equal to the theoretical value with zero decimal deviation is suspicious. All real mass spectrometers have non-zero measurement error.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Non-zero mass error between 0.01 and 0.5 Da typical depending on instrument type
red_flags: Observed mass exactly equals theoretical mass with zero difference to all decimal places reported
Mass error reported as exactly 0.000 Da on a high-precision instrument
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Calculate observed minus theoretical mass; flag if exactly zero with no decimal variation
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Some instrument software rounds reported mass to nearest whole Da — exact integer matches with MALDI-TOF are common; flag primarily when decimal precision is claimed alongside zero error

forgery_indicators.name: Identical COA Structure Across Different Peptide Products
forgery_indicators.description: Using the same base COA document for different peptide products — changing only the product name — is a definitively confirmed fraud pattern.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Distinct COA documents with different data values for each chemically distinct peptide product
red_flags: Same COA template with only the compound name edited
Identical retention time values appearing for multiple chemically different peptides
Identical purity value to the decimal for multiple different peptide products
Same lot number appearing for supposedly different independent products
Same PDF metadata creation timestamp for COAs from supposedly different test events
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Compare COAs across vendor's product catalog; flag when data content is structurally identical across chemically distinct compounds
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Similar COA formatting and templates across a vendor's products is normal; identical numerical data content across different compounds is not

forgery_indicators.name: Identical HPLC Retention Times Across Different Peptides
forgery_indicators.description: Different peptides have different RP-HPLC retention times due to differing physicochemical properties. Identical retention times across products indicates fabricated data.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Different retention times for chemically distinct peptide products on the same HPLC method
red_flags: Same retention time to the minute and second for multiple different peptides
Same chromatogram file reused across multiple products with only peak labels altered
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Compare reported peak retention times across vendor's different peptide COAs; flag exact matches between chemically distinct compounds
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Peptides of similar size and hydrophobicity may have similar but not identical retention times; flag only exact minute-and-second matches

forgery_indicators.name: Blurry Official Stamps or Seals With Sharp Surrounding Text
forgery_indicators.description: When lab stamps and seals appear blurry while surrounding document text is sharp, the official elements were inserted from a lower-resolution source image.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Lab stamps, seals, and signatures at consistent resolution with surrounding document text
red_flags: Lab stamp visibly blurrier than document body text at same zoom level
Official seal appears to be a photographed image inserted into digital document
Signature scan quality inconsistent with overall document quality
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Zoom to same magnification for stamp/seal areas and body text; compare relative sharpness
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Physical rubber stamps scanned to PDF may legitimately appear at lower resolution than surrounding digital text — evaluate in context of overall document origin

forgery_indicators.name: Localized Pixelation in Specific Data Fields
forgery_indicators.description: When only specific data fields (especially purity values or mass results) appear pixelated while surrounding text is clean, those values were digitally altered after document creation.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Uniform rendering quality for all text elements within each section of the results table
red_flags: Purity number appears pixelated while column header directly above is crisp
MS mass value at visibly different resolution than surrounding results table text
Any individual data field showing localized pixelation while neighbors are sharp
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Zoom to 400% in each result field; compare rendering quality of data values to surrounding labels and headers in same table
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: PDF rendering artifacts from certain low-quality PDF viewers can affect specific text glyphs; verify observation across multiple PDF readers before concluding

forgery_indicators.name: Generic or Suspiciously Sequential Batch Numbers
forgery_indicators.description: Legitimate batch numbers follow production-meaningful formats. Generic placeholder or trivially sequential batch numbers suggest data fabrication.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Batch numbers with date components, meaningful alphanumeric sequences, or other non-trivially-sequential formats
red_flags: Batch numbers in simple sequential series like 'BATCH-001', 'BATCH-002', 'BATCH-003'
Single-digit batch numbers
Batch number identical to batch number on a different product's COA
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Analyze batch number format; compare batch numbers across vendor's different products; flag obvious sequential counting patterns
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Simple sequential numbering is sometimes used by small legitimate vendors; evaluate in context of other indicators

forgery_indicators.name: Entire Vendor Catalog Shows Consistently Perfect Purity
forgery_indicators.description: A vendor's entire catalog showing 99%+ purity without any natural variation is statistically implausible. Real synthesis and testing produces natural variation across batches.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Natural variation in purity across product catalog (typical range 97.5%–99.8%)
red_flags: Every product across vendor's catalog shows exactly 99%+ purity
No product in catalog ever tests below 98.5%
Purity values suspiciously uniform across diverse peptides of different synthesis complexity
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Aggregate purity data across vendor's full catalog; calculate variance; flag near-zero variance as biologically implausible
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: High-quality vendors with consistent manufacturing can legitimately achieve high average purity; evaluate variance in combination with other indicators

forgery_indicators.name: COA Available Only After Purchase
forgery_indicators.description: Legitimate vendors post COAs publicly or provide them pre-purchase on request. Withholding COA until after payment prevents pre-purchase verification.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: COA accessible pre-purchase: either posted publicly on product page or provided promptly on pre-purchase request
red_flags: COA only provided after order is placed and paid
COA locked behind mandatory account creation requiring purchase
Vendor refuses or ignores pre-purchase COA requests
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Attempt to access or request COA before placing any order; note whether request is fulfilled
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Some vendors require a simple email request to receive COA — acceptable if fulfilled promptly and completely without requiring purchase

forgery_indicators.name: Vendor Discourages Direct Lab Contact
forgery_indicators.description: A vendor who discourages or prevents buyers from contacting the testing laboratory directly is attempting to prevent authentic verification.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Vendor actively supports or at minimum does not obstruct direct lab verification
red_flags: Vendor explicitly states 'do not contact the lab directly'
Vendor removes or obscures lab contact information from COA copies
Vendor response becomes defensive, evasive, or hostile when direct lab verification is requested
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Request lab contact information from vendor; note response character and whether lab contact is supported
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Labs may have confidentiality agreements preventing vendor identity disclosure — vendor can legitimately decline to share their client relationship while the lab can still confirm the report number independently

forgery_indicators.name: Independent Community Testing Contradicts COA Claims
forgery_indicators.description: When independent buyer or community retesting of a vendor's products consistently contradicts the COA purity claims, the COA is unreliable regardless of appearance.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Independent community testing results consistent with COA purity claims
red_flags: Multiple independent community tests finding substantially lower purity than COA states
Independent testing finding a different compound than what COA identifies
Multiple buyers reporting receiving mislabeled product despite plausible-looking COA
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Search community testing databases and forums for independent test results for this specific vendor; require multiple consistent independent reports before treating as definitive
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Community testing quality varies; isolated reports may reflect storage or handling issues; require multiple consistent independent data points

forgery_indicators.name: Lab Has No Record of Vendor or Batch When Contacted
forgery_indicators.description: When the testing laboratory is contacted directly, they deny any knowledge of the vendor or the specific batch number on the COA.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Lab can locate and confirm the existence of the specified test report or task number
red_flags: Lab states they have never worked with the named vendor
Lab has no record of the specified batch, task, or report number
Lab explicitly denies issuing the document
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Contact lab directly using information on COA; provide COA report/task number; ask lab to confirm existence of that specific report
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Labs may not disclose client identity lists but should be able to confirm or deny the existence of a specific report number in their system

forgery_indicators.name: Vendor Marketing Contains RUO Compliance Red Flags
forgery_indicators.description: Vendor marketing that makes therapeutic claims, provides dosing protocols, or sells injection supplies alongside RUO peptides indicates non-compliant operation that correlates with documentation fraud risk.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Vendor uses strictly research-oriented language; no therapeutic or clinical claims; no human use implications
red_flags: Anti-aging, fat-burning, or performance-enhancement language in product descriptions
Dosing protocols or cycle recommendations in product listings
Sells syringes, bacteriostatic water, or injection supplies as primary product bundling
Customer review section describing physiological effects in human users
Social media lifestyle marketing showing human use
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Review vendor website language, product descriptions, and social media; flag therapeutic or human-use language
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Some vendors sell both RUO peptides and legitimate non-peptide products; evaluate specifically for therapeutic claims about the RUO peptides themselves

forgery_indicators.name: Pricing Dramatically Below Market Rate
forgery_indicators.description: Pricing significantly below the established market rate for verified vendors may indicate substandard, misrepresented, or counterfeit product.
forgery_indicators.expected_value: Pricing within reasonable competitive range of comparable verified vendors (±40% of market median)
red_flags: Price is 50%+ below the established market rate without any explained reason
Dramatically lower pricing combined with other COA red flags
forgery_indicators.evaluation_method: Compare price to established market rate from verified vendors on price-comparison platforms; flag significant deviations
forgery_indicators.false_positive_notes: Legitimate discounts (bulk orders, promotional codes, new-vendor pricing) can explain price differences; pricing alone without other red flags is a very weak indicator

calibration_method: Empirical fake-vs-orig flag-rate discrimination on 100+100 sample from coa-faker output. Non-evaluable rules retain original weight.
